schema.description: Use this data source to retrieve information about VKCS Kubernetes cluster.

string.description: The region in which the cluster resides. If omitted, the provider's configured `region` is used.
The cluster's ID.
The cluster's UUID.
The user-assigned name of the cluster.
The installed Kubernetes version (e.g., `v1.34.2`).

bool.description: Indicates whether a floating IP is assigned to the cluster's API endpoint.

string.description: A user-provided description of the cluster.
The ID of the VPC network where the cluster is deployed.
The ID of the subnet used by the cluster's nodes.

set.description: The availability zones in which the master nodes reside.

string.description: The cluster type: `standard` (single AZ) or `regional` (three AZs).
The ID of the external network providing internet access to the cluster.

set.description: A list of container registry addresses that can be accessed without certificate validation.

string.description: The CNI network plugin in use (always `calico`).
The IPv4 CIDR block allocated for pod networking.

map.description: Kubernetes labels applied to the entire cluster.

string.description: The ID of the subnet where LoadBalancer type services are provisioned.

set.description: CIDR blocks permitted to access the cluster's API load balancer.

int64.description: The number of master nodes (1, 3, or 5) ensuring etcd quorum.

string.description: The flavor ID (VM type) used for master nodes.

set_nested.description: Information about the disks attached to master nodes (root, etcd, etcd-events). Each object contains `type` (disk type) and `size` (GB).

string.description: The storage volume type used for this disk (e.g., `ceph-ssd`, `ceph-hdd`). Corresponds to the available volume types in the region.

int64.description: The allocated size of the disk in gigabytes (GB).

string.description: The raw kubeconfig file contents used to authenticate to the Kubernetes API.
The timestamp when the cluster was created (ISO 8601 format).
The current operational status of the cluster (e.g., `RUNNING`, `RECONCILING`).
The VK Cloud project ID owning the cluster.
The floating IP address of the API load balancer (present only when `public_ip` is `true`).
The internal virtual IP address of the API load balancer.
The full URL of the Kubernetes API server (e.g., `https://api.example.com:6443`).

set_nested.description: A list of node groups associated with the cluster. Each object includes `id`, `name`, `flavor`, `node_count`, and `availability_zone`.

string.description: The unique identifier of the node group.
The user-assigned name of the node group.
The flavor ID (VM type) used for each worker node in this group.

int64.description: The current number of worker nodes in the group. This value reflects the actual node count, accounting for both manual scaling and autoscaling actions.

string.description: The availability zone where the nodes of this group are deployed.

schema.description: Use this data source to retrieve information about VKCS Kubernetes cluster node group.

string.description: The region where the node group resides. Defaults to the provider's `region`.
The ID of the node group to retrieve.
The node group's UUID
The user-assigned name of the node group.
The ID of the parent Kubernetes cluster.
The flavor ID (VM type) of each node in the group.
The availability zone where the nodes are located.
The scaling strategy: `fixed_scale` (static node count) or `auto_scale` (dynamic based on load).

int64.description: The desired number of nodes when `scale_type` is `fixed_scale`.
The current number of nodes when `scale_type` is `auto_scale`.
The minimum allowed node count for `auto_scale`.
The maximum allowed node count for `auto_scale`.

map.description: Kubernetes labels applied to the nodes in this group.

set_nested.description: Taints applied to the nodes. Each taint contains `key`, `value`, and `effect` (e.g., `NoSchedule`).

string.description: The taint key. Must conform to Kubernetes label key syntax (e.g., `node.kubernetes.io/unreachable`).
The taint value. Must conform to Kubernetes label value syntax (e.g., `production`).
The effect of the taint. Allowed values are: `NoSchedule`, `PreferNoSchedule`, `NoExecute`.

int64.description: The maximum percentage of nodes that can be upgraded simultaneously (1-100).

string.description: The type of root volume attached to each node (e.g., `ceph-ssd`).

int64.description: The size of the root volume in GB.

string.description: The creation timestamp of the node group (ISO 8601 format).

schema.description: Use this data source to retrieve information about VKCS Kubernetes cluster versions.

string.description: The region for which to retrieve available versions. Defaults to provider's `region`.
A synthetic identifier set to "kubernetes_versions". This data source does not have a natural ID.

set.description: A set of available Kubernetes version strings (e.g., ["v1.33.3", "v1.34.2"]).

schema.description: Use this data source to retrieve information about available VKCS Kubernetes node group root volume types.

string.description: The region for which to retrieve volume types. Defaults to provider's `region`.
A synthetic identifier set to "volume_types". This data source does not have a natural ID.

set_nested.description: A set of available volume types with their supported availability zones.

string.description: The name of the volume type (e.g., "ceph-hdd", "high-iops").

set.description: A set of availability zones where this volume type is available (e.g., ["PA2", "MS1", "ME1"]).

schema.description: Use this data source to retrieve information about available VKCS Kubernetes cluster security policy templates.

string.description: A synthetic identifier set to "policy_templates". This data source does not have a natural ID.
The region for which to retrieve security policy templates. Defaults to provider's `region`.

set_nested.description: A set of available security policy templates. Each template contains an `id`, `name`, `description`, `settings_description`, and `version`.

string.description: Unique identifier of the security policy template.
Name of the security policy template (e.g., `k8sallowedrepos`).
Brief description of the template's purpose.
Base64-encoded JSON schema defining the configurable parameters of the policy.
Version of the security policy template.

schema.description: Use this data source to retrieve information about specific VKCS Kubernetes cluster security policy template.

string.description: The region for which to retrieve security policy templates. Defaults to provider's `region`.
The security policy template's ID. Used to fetch a specific security policy template.
Name of the security policy template (e.g., `k8sallowedrepos`).
Version of the security policy template.
Brief description of the template's purpose.
Base64-encoded JSON schema defining the configurable parameters of the policy.

schema.description: Use this data source to retrieve information about available VKCS Kubernetes addons.

string.description: A synthetic identifier for the data source.
The region for which to retrieve list of addons. Defaults to provider's `region`.
Unique identifier of the addon.
Human-readable name of the addon.

list_nested.description: List of available versions for the addon.

string.description: Unique identifier of the addon version.
Version string of the addon.

schema.description: Use this data source to retrieve information about specific VKCS Kubernetes addon.

string.description: The region for which to retrieve specific addon. Defaults to provider's `region`.
Unique identifier of the addon version.
Name of the addon.
Version of the addon.
Identifier of the addon this version belongs to.
Base64-encoded values.yaml template for the addon.

schema.description: Use this data source to retrieve information about specific VKCS Kubernetes cluster addon.

string.description: The region in which to get the cluster addon. If omitted, the provider's `region` is used.
The unique identifier of the cluster addon.
The ID of the Kubernetes cluster where the addon is installed.
The human-readable name of the Kubernetes addon (e.g., `ingress-nginx`, `argocd`).
The unique identifier of the addon definition.
The ID of the addon version to install.
The Kubernetes namespace where the addon will be deployed.
The Helm chart values for configuring the addon, typically provided as YAML string.
The human-readable name of the cluster addon.
The current deployment status of the addon (e.g., 'INSTALLING', 'UPDATING', 'DELETING').
The timestamp when the addon was last updated.
The timestamp when the addon was created.

schema.description: Provides a Kubernetes cluster resource. This can be used to create, modify and delete Kubernetes clusters.

single_nested.description: Timeout configuration for create, update and delete operations.

string.description: Timeout for create operation (e.g., `60m`, `2h`). Default is `60m`.
Timeout for update operation (e.g., `120m`, `3h`). Default is `60m`.
Timeout for delete operation (e.g., `120m`, `3h`). Default is `30m`.

custom_type.type: timeouts.Type{ObjectType: types.ObjectType{AttrTypes: map[string]attr.Type{"create": types.StringType, "update": types.StringType, "delete": types.StringType}}}

string.description: The region in which to create the cluster. If omitted, the provider's `region` is used. **Forces replacement** on change.
The unique identifier of the cluster.
The UUID of the cluster. If omitted, it is generated automatically. **Forces replacement** on change.
The Kubernetes version to deploy. Only upgrades to a higher version are allowed.

bool.description: If `true`, assigns a floating IP to the API endpoint. When enabled, `external_network_id` must be specified. **Forces replacement** on change. Default is `false`.

string.description: A unique cluster name. Must be 3-25 lowercase alphanumeric characters or hyphens, start and end with an alphanumeric character, and must not contain consecutive hyphens. **Forces replacement** on change.
A human-readable description of the cluster. The maximum length is 256 characters. **Forces replacement** on change.
The ID of the network in which the cluster will be deployed. The network must use the `sprut` SDN. **Forces replacement** on change.
The ID of the subnet in which the cluster worker nodes will be deployed. **Forces replacement** on change.

set.description: A set of availability zones for the cluster masters. A `standard` cluster must specify one zone, while a `regional` cluster must specify three zones. **Forces replacement** on change.

string.description: Either `standard` or `regional`. A `standard` cluster uses a single availability zone, while a `regional` cluster uses three availability zones. **Forces replacement** on change.
The ID of the external network used for internet access. If omitted, one is selected automatically. **Forces replacement** on change.

set.description: A set of container registry hosts (e.g., `myregistry.com`) that can be accessed without TLS verification. **Forces replacement** on change.

string.description: The CNI plugin to use. Currently, only `calico` is supported. **Forces replacement** on change.
The IPv4 CIDR block used for the pod network. Must be a valid private CIDR (e.g., `10.100.0.0/16`). **Forces replacement** on change.

map.description: A map of Kubernetes labels applied to the cluster. Keys and values must conform to Kubernetes label syntax. **Forces replacement** on change.

string.description: The ID of the subnet used for allocating IP addresses for LoadBalancer services. **Forces replacement** on change.

set.description: A set of CIDR blocks allowed to access the API load balancer. If empty, access is allowed from all IP addresses. **Forces replacement** on change.

int64.description: The number of master nodes. Must be an odd number (1, 3, or 5) to maintain etcd quorum. **Forces replacement** on change.

string.description: The flavor ID used for master nodes. Changing this triggers a rolling upgrade of the control plane.

set_nested.description: Information about the master node disks.

string.description: The storage volume type for this master disk.

int64.description: The size of the disk in gigabytes (GB).

string.description: The contents of the kubeconfig file. **Sensitive** — store securely.
The time when the cluster was created.
The current status of the cluster (e.g., `RUNNING`, `RECONCILING`).
The ID of the project that owns the cluster.
The floating IP of the API load balancer, present only if `public_ip` is `true`.
The internal virtual IP (VIP) of the API load balancer.
The Kubernetes API server URL.

set_nested.description: A list of node groups associated with the cluster.

string.description: The ID of the node group.
The name of the node group.
The flavor ID used for worker nodes.

int64.description: The current number of nodes in the node group.

string.description: The availability zone where the node group is located.

schema.description: Provides a Kubernetes cluster node group resource. This can be used to create, modify, and delete Kubernetes cluster node group.

single_nested.description: Timeout configuration for create, update and delete operations.

string.description: Timeout for create operation (e.g., `60m`, `2h`). Default is `30m`.
Timeout for update operation (e.g., `120m`, `3h`). Default is `30m`.
Timeout for delete operation (e.g., `120m`, `3h`). Default is `30m`.

custom_type.type: timeouts.Type{ObjectType: types.ObjectType{AttrTypes: map[string]attr.Type{"create": types.StringType, "update": types.StringType, "delete": types.StringType}}}

string.description: The region where the node group will be created. If omitted, the provider's `region` is used. **Forces replacement** on change.
The unique identifier of the node group.
The ID of the parent cluster. **Forces replacement** on change.
The UUID of the node group. It is generated automatically.
A unique name for the node group within the cluster. Must be 3-25 characters, lowercase alphanumeric and hyphens only. **Forces replacement** on change.
The flavor ID used for worker nodes. Changing this triggers a rolling upgrade of the nodes.
The availability zone where the nodes will be placed. **Forces replacement** on change.
Type of scaling for the node group. Must be either `fixed_scale` or `auto_scale`. If `scale_type` is `auto_scale`, the condition `auto_scale_min_size` <= `auto_scale_max_size` must be met.

int64.description: The desired number of nodes. Minimum value is 0. This argument is required when `scale_type` is `fixed_scale`.
The current number of nodes in the node group. Only applicable when `scale_type` is `auto_scale`. This value is computed by the provider and cannot be set by the user.
The minimum number of nodes the autoscaler can scale down to. Must be at least 0. Required when `scale_type` is `auto_scale`.
The maximum number of nodes the autoscaler may scale out to. Must be greater than or equal to 0 and also greater than or equal to `auto_scale_min_size`. Required if scale_type is `auto_scale`.

map.description: Kubernetes labels to apply to the nodes in this node group. Both keys and values must conform to Kubernetes label syntax.

set_nested.description: Kubernetes taints to apply to the nodes in this node group. Each taint must specify `key`, `value`, and `effect` — where `effect` is one of: `NoSchedule`, `PreferNoSchedule`, `NoExecute`.

string.description: The key of the taint. Must conform to Kubernetes label key syntax.
The value of the taint. Must conform to Kubernetes label value syntax.
The effect of the taint. Must be one of: `NoSchedule`, `PreferNoSchedule`, `NoExecute`.

int64.description: The maximum percentage of nodes (1-100) that can be upgraded simultaneously during a rolling upgrade.

string.description: The root volume type. For example: `ceph-ssd`. Use the `vkcs_kubernetes_volume_types_v2` data source to list available types for the selected availability zone. **Forces replacement** on change.

int64.description: The size of the root volume, in gigabytes (GB). Must be at least 1. **Forces replacement** on change.

string.description: The timestamp when the node group was created, in ISO 8601 format.

schema.description: Provides a Kubernetes cluster security policy resource. This can be used to create, modify, and delete Kubernetes cluster security policy.

string.description: The region in which to create the Kubernetes security policy. If omitted, the provider's `region` is used. **Forces replacement** on change.
The ID of the cluster security policy.
The ID of the Kubernetes cluster. **Forces replacement** on change.
The ID of the security policy template. **Forces replacement** on change.
Namespace to apply security policy to. Changing this updates the security policy.
Policy settings. Changing this updates the security policy.

bool.description: Controls whether the security policy is enabled. Default is `true`. Changing this updates the security policy.

schema.description: Provides a Kubernetes cluster addon resource. This can be used to create, modify, and delete Kubernetes cluster addon.

single_nested.description: Timeout configuration for create, update and delete operations.

string.description: Timeout for create operation (e.g., `60m`, `2h`). Default is `20m`.
Timeout for update operation (e.g., `120m`, `3h`). Default is `20m`.
Timeout for delete operation (e.g., `120m`, `3h`). Default is `20m`.

custom_type.type: timeouts.Type{ObjectType: types.ObjectType{AttrTypes: map[string]attr.Type{"create": types.StringType, "update": types.StringType, "delete": types.StringType}}}

string.description: The region in which to create the cluster addon. If omitted, the provider's `region` is used. **Forces replacement** on change.
The unique identifier of the cluster addon.
The ID of the Kubernetes cluster where the addon will be installed. **Forces replacement** on change.
The unique identifier of the addon definition. **Forces replacement** on change.
The ID of the addon version to install. **Forces replacement** on change.
The Kubernetes namespace where the addon will be deployed. Must be a valid DNS subdomain name (e.g., 'my-namespace'). **Forces replacement** on change.
The Helm chart values for configuring the addon, provided as YAML string.
The human-readable name of the cluster addon. **Forces replacement** on change.
The current deployment status of the addon (e.g., 'INSTALLING', 'UPDATING', 'DELETING').
The timestamp when the addon was last updated.
The timestamp when the addon was created.
